subject: Physics
question: A block is pulled by two forces: $F_1 = 3\text{ N}$ North and $F_2 = 4\text{ N}$ East. What is the magnitude of the resultant force?

poll_explanation.why: Because the East and North forces act perpendicular ($90^\circ$) to one another, they form a right triangle.\par Substituting components yields:\par $R = \sqrt{3^2 + 4^2} = \sqrt{9 + 16} = \sqrt{25} = 5\text{ N}$.
poll_explanation.poll_hint: R = √(F1² + F2²) = √(3² + 4²) = √25 = 5 N. Perpendicular vector components follow Pythagorean theorem.

options_analysis.why: Correct. Application of standard Pythagorean theorem on vector components.
Incorrect. This represents standard algebraic addition, which only applies to parallel collinear vectors.
Incorrect. This represents collinear subtraction, which only applies to anti-parallel vectors pointing in opposite directions.
Incorrect. This represents the vector magnitude product, which is not physically valid for vector addition.
